# Copilot Instructions for gh-summary

This file helps a coding agent work efficiently with this repository.

## Key Documentation

> **Note**: There is no `AGENTS.md`, `ACCESSIBILITY.md`, or `SUSTAINABILITY.md` in this repository yet.

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| [README.md](../README.md) | Project overview, features, CLI usage, and deployment steps |

| [CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md](../CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) | Community standards |

## Repository Overview

**gh-summary** is a Node.js tool (no npm dependencies) that fetches and visualizes GitHub user or organization activity. It has two modes:

1. **CLI tools** – `gh-summary.mjs` (user), `gh-org-summary.mjs` (organisation).
2. **GitHub Pages web app** – static site served from the repository root, with cached data in `_data/`.

### File Map

```
gh-summary/
├── gh-summary.mjs          # CLI: summarise a user's activity
├── gh-org-summary.mjs      # CLI: summarise an organisation's activity
├── generate-data.mjs       # Generates _data/activity.json + _data/metadata.json
├── generate-usage.mjs      # Generates usage/statistics data
├── config.yml              # Project configuration (default_user, schedule, privacy)
├── index.html              # Web interface HTML
├── app.js                  # Web interface JavaScript
├── styles.css              # Web interface styles
├── usage.html / usage.js   # Usage statistics page
├── .nojekyll               # Disables Jekyll on GitHub Pages (do not remove)
├── _data/
│   ├── activity.json       # Cached activity for the default user
│   └── metadata.json       # Metadata about the last data generation run
└── .github/
    └── workflows/
        ├── generate-data.yml   # Scheduled data generation (15th + last day of month)
        └── pages.yml           # GitHub Pages deployment
```

## How to Run / Test

There is no `package.json`; the project uses Node.js built-ins only (Node 18+ required for native `fetch`).

```bash
# CLI – user summary
node gh-summary.mjs --user <github-username> --days 7

# CLI – organisation summary
node gh-org-summary.mjs --org <org-name> --days 7

# Regenerate cached data (writes to _data/)
node generate-data.mjs

# Serve the web interface locally
python3 -m http.server 8000
# Then open http://localhost:8000
```

Set `GITHUB_TOKEN` in the environment to avoid rate-limiting and to include aggregated private-repo statistics.

## Important Conventions

- **Indentation**: 2 spaces in JavaScript, HTML, and CSS.
- **No build step**: All JS/CSS is vanilla and served as-is; do not introduce a bundler.
- **No npm packages**: The project is intentionally dependency-free.
- **GitHub API**: Always include a `User-Agent` header in API requests to avoid 403 errors.
- **Privacy-first**: Never expose private repository names, commit messages, or PR titles. Only aggregated counts are allowed for private activity (e.g., "5 commits in private repositories" — no names or content).
- **URL parameters**: Use `URLSearchParams` and `history.pushState` for URL state management in `app.js`.
- **Error display**: System-level errors go into the `#system-messages` collapsible element at the bottom of the page, not prominently at the top.
- **Single user display**: Only one user's data is shown at a time; when `?u=` is present in the URL the `#default-user-section` is hidden.
- **Data generation**: Activity data is generated by `generate-data.mjs` and stored in `_data/activity.json`. The scheduled workflow runs on the 15th and last day of each month.
- **`.nojekyll`**: This file must remain in the repository root to disable Jekyll processing on GitHub Pages.

## Configuration

All project settings live in `config.yml`:

```yaml
default_user: mgifford        # GitHub username whose data is cached
schedule:
  frequency: bimonthly        # 'bimonthly' or 'monthly'
  timezone: America/Toronto
activity:
  days: 31
  include_private_stats: true
  include_types: []           # empty = all types
privacy:
  aggregate_only: true
  allow_user_queries: true
```

## Errors & Known Workarounds

- **GitHub API 403**: Caused by missing `User-Agent` header. All fetch calls to `api.github.com` must include `'User-Agent': 'gh-summary'` (or similar).
- **Default-user data load failure**: Hide `#default-user-section` and surface the error in `#system-messages` (see `app.js`).
- **GitHub Pages 404 after first deploy**: Wait 5–10 minutes; the `.nojekyll` file must be present.
- **No data showing on site**: Run `node generate-data.mjs` locally, commit `_data/`, and push; or trigger the "Generate Activity Data" workflow manually from the Actions tab.
